Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • CIVIL ENGINEERING • Intermediate • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

It takes six technicians a total of 10 hour to build a new server from direct, computer, with each working at the same rate. If six technicians starts to build the server at 10 AM, and one technician per hour is added beginning at 4 PM, at what time will the server be completed?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Total amount of work = 60 man-hours
From 10 AM to 4 pm, 6 technicians = 36 – man hours
From 4 PM to 5 PM, 7 technicians = 7 man hours
From 5 PM to 6 PM, 8 technicians = 8 man hours
From 6 PM to 7 PM, 9 technicians = 9 man-hours
Total = 60 man hours

Q7 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • CIVIL ENGINEERING • Intermediate • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Choose the missing term to complete the given series
3, 5, 10, 12, 24, 26, 52, ?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
3 × 1 + 2 = 5
5 × 2 + 0 = 10
10 × 1 + 2 = 12
12 × 2 + 0 = 24
24 × 1 + 2 = 26
26 × 2 + 0 = 52
52 × 1 + 2 = 54

Not attempted

Consider the following code:

What is the functionality of above function Do ( )?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Here two pointers are used gate1 and gate2. gate1 pointer points to beginning and gate2 points to the end. Loop is set up that compares the characters pointed by these two pointers. If the characters do not match, then it’s returning 0 i.e. it is not a palindrome. It returns1 for both even and odd palindrome (i.e., when match occurs for entire loop).

Q32 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following three concurrent processes:

A and B are two counting semaphore variable and P and V are usual semaphore operation if A = 0, B = 4 what is the minimum number of 0’s printed by the execution of the above processes ________.

NAT
Correct answer: 0
Solution

0
(i) A = 0, B = 4
Process 0 execute 4 times and blocked, now value of A = 4.
(ii) A = 4, B = 0
Process P2 execute 4 time and blocked, now value of A = 0, B = 0.
(iii) Process 1 execute P(A) and blocked, number of times 0 is printed is 0.

Q33 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) Johnson counter is a self decoding circuit.
(b) Number of unused states is Johnson counter is 2n – n.
(c) Ripple carry adder has addition time independent of the number of bits.
(d) Carry look ahead is used to speed up parallel addition.
Which of the above statements is/are true?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• Number of unused state in Johnson counter = 2n – 2n.
• In ripple carry adder, addition time is dependent of the number of bits. So option (c) is incorrect.

Q36 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• CLR(1) parsers SR conflicts can occur.
• Every regular language is LL(1) is true. Because atleast there exist a regular grammar which is LL(1).
• An ambiguous grammar can never be LR(K) for any K.
• If a grammar is LR(0) then it will always be SLR(1). It implies that if no conflict is present in LR(0) then SLR(1) also don’t have any kind of conflict.

Q44 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) Dijkstra’s algorithm works on any graph without negative cycles.
(b) Dijkstra’s algorithm shortest path may relax an edge more than once in a graph with a cycle.
(c) Every directed acyclic graph has exactly one topological ordering.
(d) Any dynamic programming algorithm with n subproblems will run in Ο(n) time.
Which of the above statements is incorrect?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
• Dijkstra’s Algorithm a single negative edges makes false the assumption that when you expand a node, you have already found the shortest path to that node. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Dijkstra’s algorithm always visit each nodes at most once, this is why it produces an incorrect result in the presence of negative weight edges. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Some directed acyclic graph can have multiple topological ordering. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• The sub problem may take longer than constant time to compute, as was the case with longest increasing subsequence. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q46 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is correct about lightweight process?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
In computer operating systems, a Light-Weight Process (LWP) is a means of achieving multitasking.
Many systems implement either the many-to-many or the two-level model place an
intermediate data structure between the user and Kernel threads. This data structure is known as
a light weight process.
• Each light weight process is attached to a Kernel thread, and it is Kernel threads that the operating system schedules to run on physical processors.
• The LWP blocks as well, if a Kernel thread blocks (such as while waiting for an operation to complete). The user-level thread attached to the LWP up the chain also blocks.

Q54 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following schema:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Inner sub query identifies all the employees in a particular department (emp_DeptID) whose salary is less than 50000. It retrieve emp_ID for those employee where the emp_DepID match the dept_ID from the outer query.
• Outer query select the dept_name from the department relation. The condition is based on the NOT EXISTS clause, which means the outer query will only return department for which the inner query finds no employees with a salary less than 50,000. So, all employee earn 50,000.
So, option (a) is correct.

Q59 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements and identify the true statements.

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• The branch target stores the previous target address for the current branch, other algorithm for branch prediction also exists. So, option (a) is correct.
• Control hazards are caused due to branched instructions. So, option (b) is correct.
• When all stages of pipeline having same delay and buffer latency is zero, for a single instruction execution time of pipeline CPU is equal to the execution time of non-pipelined CPU.
• For a single instruction time taken on pipelined CPU is greater than or equal to the identical non-pipelined.

Q63 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• A Lattice is bounded iff the Lattice has a greatest and a least element.
• A finite Lattice is always bounded.
• Complement lattice is defined only for bounded lattice. A bounded lattice is complemented iff alteast one complement of every element exists in lattice. An element should one or more complements.
• Every cyclic group is Abelian group but every Abelian group is not cyclic group.

Q64 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) A be skew-symmetric matrix of odd order, then determinant is zero.
(b) Maximum number of distinct eigen values of matrix A < size of matrix.
(c) For all 3 × 3 matrices A and B, AB = BA
(d) If you have an m × m matrix A that is invertible then, the column of A form a basis for Rm.
Which of the above statement is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• Skew symmetric matrix is odd order then determinant is always zero. So, option (a) is correct.
• Maximum number of distinct eigen values = size of matrix A. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Matrix multiplication is not commutative thus, AB ≠ BA. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Am × m which is invertible thus |A| ≠ 0 i.e., linear combinations of columns space of A covers the whole Rm. So, option (d) is correct.

Q65 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In a system, the L1 cache has 75% hit rate and takes 4 cycles on a hit, L2 cache has a 85% hit rate with a 15 cycles access time. The main memory access time is 150 cycles. If the main memory speed is improved by 20%. What is the improvement factor in L1 miss time? (Upto 2 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 1.14 [1.10 - 1.20]
Solution

1.14 [1.10 - 1.20]
